fact: May 23 is exactly 143 days into the year - that's like counting to 143 Mississippi's without stopping!
In the Northern Hemisphere, dandelions are going absolutely bonkers right now, shooting their fluffy seed parachutes everywhere like nature's confetti cannons.
The sun rises before 6 AM in most of North America on this date, which means even sleepyheads get extra morning light whether they want it or not.
Gemini season is in full swing - people born around now are supposed to have two personalities, like having a built-in best friend who never leaves.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, May 23 falls smack in the middle of autumn, so leaves are turning colors while everyone up north is planting gardens.
Cicadas in many regions are starting to emerge from underground after spending years as chunky grubs - imagine sleeping underground for 17 years and waking up with wings!
This date sits exactly 222 days away from the end of the year, making it a perfect mathematical middle point that math nerds secretly celebrate.
In northern countries like Canada, black flies are reaching peak annoyance levels around now - they're so tiny but so incredibly good at ruining picnics.
The moon can appear anywhere from a tiny sliver to completely full on May 23, depending on the year, but it always looks extra bright against the late spring sky.
Strawberry plants in temperate regions are forming their first tiny green berries right about now - they look like miniature green brains before turning red and delicious.
May 23 falls during the time when Earth is tilting toward summer in the north, making shadows shorter and giving everything that golden, warm glow that makes you want to stay outside forever.
In many places, this is prime tornado season - the atmosphere gets so mixed up between hot and cold air that it literally starts spinning like a blender.
